displayTitle: Mass, Homogeneity, and the Level II Call

beats.narration: A coil never tests a point. It samples a volume of metal, and two part factors move the signal before any flaw does.

payload.title: MASS AND HOMOGENEITY
payload.sub: What the part itself does to your eddy current signal.

beats.narration: The mass effect is how much conductive metal sits in the field. Thicker than three delta looks infinite; thinner, and wall thickness becomes a variable.
Homogeneity is the second factor. Segregation, heat treatment, hardness, grain — they all shift local conductivity and drift the dot along the conductivity locus.
On the impedance plane each writes a signature. Conductivity drifts ride the locus, thinning unwinds toward air, and a crack cuts across, phase-separated from lift-off.

payload.heading: Every effect has a direction
payload.note: DRIFT ALONG LOCUS = MATERIAL · CUT ACROSS = FLAW

beats.narration: Material varying, or metal missing? Gradual along the locus is a zone. Abrupt, footprint-wide, phase-separated is the flaw. That call is the lesson.

payload.sub: Discriminate by onset, direction, and phase.
